name: ToolJet API
what_it_does: ToolJet — open-source, self-hostable low-code platform for building internal tools and admin panels with drag-and-drop UI builder, 50+ data source connectors, and a REST API for workflow automation.
use_cases: Agents triggering ToolJet workflows to execute multi-step internal automation across connected data sources
Data management UI — agents building CRUD interfaces on top of databases, APIs, and SaaS tools without frontend code
Workflow automation — agents running ToolJet workflows that sequence database queries, API calls, and transformations
Internal dashboard creation — agents programmatically creating apps and dashboards for internal reporting
Self-hosted alternative to Retool — agents using ToolJet's open-source platform in air-gapped environments
not_for: Customer-facing applications — ToolJet is for internal team tools, not public-facing products
Complex UI requirements — ToolJet's drag-and-drop has limitations for highly custom interfaces
Teams needing commercial SLA support — open-source self-hosted version has no guaranteed support
best_when: You need an open-source, self-hosted alternative to Retool for building internal tools with full data control and no per-user licensing fees.
avoid_when: You need a fully managed, commercially supported internal tool platform — use Retool for enterprise SLA.

auth.notes: JWT-based authentication. Personal access tokens for API access. SSO via Google, GitHub, and LDAP. SAML available on Enterprise plan.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted: free forever, unlimited users; Cloud Free: 5 users
pricing.paid_tiers: Cloud Business: $20/user/month; Enterprise: custom with SSO and audit logs
pricing.notes: Open source under AGPL — self-hosted is free for teams. Cloud tier for managed hosting. Enterprise adds SAML, audit logs, and SLA.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no limits; Cloud: plan-dependent workflow execution limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documentation is less comprehensive than commercial alternatives. Error messages are functional but sometimes require log inspection. Workflow execution errors need UI review.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Workflow execution is not idempotent — each trigger creates a new run. App configuration updates are idempotent with same settings.
known_agent_gotchas: API documentation is community-maintained and may lag behind feature releases
Workflow IDs are opaque UUIDs — agents must query app list to discover workflow IDs
Data source connections are workspace-level — agents cannot create new connections via REST API
Self-hosted ToolJet version may differ from cloud — API compatibility must be verified per deployment
AGPL license means modifications to ToolJet server must be open-sourced if distributed

security_score_components.security_notes: AGPL open source — auditable. Self-hosted for data sovereignty. SOC2 for cloud. Secrets encrypted at rest. SSO integration via SAML/OIDC on Enterprise.
